TipMetadata
TipLog
  • 10 Jun. 2026 - ecTools workflow; GROFTET and area basis from ArealGrunnlag_Groftet.xlsx
  • 03 Jul. 2026 - Update calculation of indicator using GROFTET and area basis from ArealGrunnlag_Groftet_Drenerbart_areal.csv

1. Summary

  1. Background and rationale:

In forested peatlands, swamp forests, and moist forests on mineral soils, species composition is influenced by a continuously high water table. Forest drainage or ditching lowers the water table in such forests, resulting in changes in the composition of forest-floor vegetation, from dominance of species adapted to moist conditions to species adapted to drier conditions. Drainage often also improves the growing conditions for trees, resulting in increased tree growth and canopy closure. This, in turn, alters the conditions for understory species through changes in light availability. This indicator represents the proportion of forest area that retains an intact water table within mesic and moist forest types where drainage is expected to affect ecosystem condition. The assessment is generally restricted to mesic and moist forests, including forested peatlands, swamp forests, but also bilberry forests, thereby excluding only very dry forest vegetation types where drainage has no or little ecological relevance. The primary anthropogenic pressure captured by the indicator is forestry drainage or ditching. The percentage/share of undrained area indicates the extent to which natural hydrological conditions have been maintained.

  1. Methods:

Data come from the National Forest Inventory (NFI). The area basis is defined by combinations of the variables veg_type and vegtype_fukt, as specified in the area basis table. The area basis for the indicator estimate is defined as forest areas where the combination of vegetation type (veg_type) and vegetation moisture type (vegtype_fukt) indicates mesic, moist or wet conditions, thereby excluding naturally dry forest types. 85% of the forest area is included in the area basis. At NFI plot level, drainage status is recorded in the NFI variable groftet (1 = ditched/drained, 2 = not ditched/ drained). The non-scaled indicator is calculated as the area-weighted share of the area with an intact water table (not drained). Non-scaled indicator values and associated uncertainty are estimated by bootstrapping county-level data, and the resulting draws are scaled and aggregated to regional and national levels.

  1. Interpretation:

An indicator value of 1 means that 100% of the assessed area is not drained and therefore has an intact water table (best condition, X100). An indicator value of 0 means that 0% of the assessed area has an intact water table (worst condition, X0).

  1. Results and development needs:

The indicator value for the last two reporting periods has been stable at 0.93 (2015-2019 and 2020-2024). There is a slight variation between regions the lowest value in East (0.89) and the highest value in North (0.97).

We have used the NFI variable groftet where only drained/not drained is recorded. There are other variables recorded in the NFI describing types of ditching and drainage effect, eventually also effects on the ground floor vegetation. These data should be evaluated for use in future versions of the indicator. Terrain slope could also be evaluated in such development. The current selection of vegetation types (veg_type) and their relative moisture condition (vegtype_fukt) should also be scrutinized. The current area basis includes 85 % of the Norwegian forest, and mainly very dry forest vegetation types have been excluded. Thus, there is potential to develop this indicator further.

1.2 Native language summary - Norwegian

  1. Bakgrunn og begrunnelse: I torvmarksskog, sumpskog og fuktig fastmarksskog er artssammensetningen formet av høy grunnvannsstand. Skogsgrøfting senker grunnvannsnivået i slike skogtyper, noe som fører til endringer i skogbunnens artssammensetning. Grøfting på slike arealer forbedrer også ofte vekstforholdene for trær, noe som kan gi økt vekst for trærne og en tettere skog. Dette påvirker igjen forholdene for vegetasjonen i feltsjiktet gjennom endret lystilgang. Indikatoren viser andelen skogareal som har et intakt grunnvannsnivå (dvs. som ikke er grøftet) innen friske og fuktige vegetasjonstyper der grøfting forventes å påvirke økosystemtilstanden. Vurderingen er avgrenset til friske og fuktige skoger, inkludert skogkledde myrer, sumpskog, men også mindre våte skogtyper som f.eks. blåbærskog. Arealutvalget inkluderer ikke naturlig tørre skogtyper der grøfting i utgangspunktet ikke har økologisk betydning. Den viktigste menneskeskapte påvirkningsfaktoren som fanges opp av indikatoren er grøfting for skogbruksformål. Andelen ugrøftet areal uttrykker i hvilken grad naturlige hydrologiske forhold er opprettholdt.

  2. Metoder: Data er hentet fra Landsskogtakseringens (LSK). Arealgrunnlaget defineres av kombinasjoner av vegetasjonstype (veg_type) og de relative fuktighetsforholdene innen disse (vegtype_fukt, jfr. area basis table). Arealgrunnlaget for beregningen er definert som skogarealer der kombinasjonen av vegetasjonstype (veg_type) og vegetasjonstypens relative fuktighetsforhold (vegtype_fukt) indikerer frisk, fuktig og våt skog. Naturlig tørre skogtyper, der grøfting ikke forventes å påvirke økosystemtilstanden, er utelatt. Totalt inngår 85% av skogarealet i arealgrunnlaget. På LSK-prøveflatenivå registreres grøfting i variabelen groftet (1 = grøftet, 2 = ikke-grøftet). Den uskalerte indikatorverdien beregnes som det arealvektede andelen av arealet med intakt grunnvannsnivå (ikke grøftet). Uskalerte indikatorverdier og tilhørende usikkerhet estimeres ved bootstrap av data på fylkesnivå. Disse skaleres og aggregeres til regionale og nasjonale indikatorverdier.

  3. Tolkning: En indikatorverdi på 1 betyr at 100 % av det vurderte arealet ikke er grøftet og dermed har et intakt grunnvannsnivå (X100). En indikatorverdi på 0 betyr at ingen andel av det vurderte arealet har et intakt grunnvannsnivå (X0).

  4. Resultater og utviklingsbehov: Indikatorverdien for de to siste rapporteringsperiodene har vært stabil på 0,93 (2015–2019 og 2020–2024). Det er en svak variasjon mellom regioner, med lavest verdi i Øst (0,89) og høyest verdi i Nord (0,97).

Indikatoren er basert på LSK-variabelen GROFTET, der arealet registreres som grøftet eller ikke grøftet. Landsskogtakseringen registrerer imidlertid flere andre variabler som beskriver grøftenes type og dreneringseffekt. Disse bør gjennomgås og benyttes i framtidig versjon av indikatoren. Også utvalget av vegetasjonstyper/skogtyper bør vurderes nærmere. Dagens arealgrunnlag omfatter 85 % av Norges skogareal, og det er hovedsakelig naturlig tørre skogtyper som er utelatt. Terrenghelning kan også vurderes som et kriterium for å utelate arealer fra beregningsgrunnlaget, i tillegg til at variablene for vegetasjonstype (VEG_TYPE) og deres fuktighetsforhold (VEGTYPE_FUKT) bør gjennomgås på nytt. Det er således et sterkt behov for å videreutvikle indikatoren.

2. About the underlying data

2.1 Spatial and temporal extent, resolution, and representativity

The indicator is generally calculated for five-year reporting periods, based on the NFI data across Norwegian forest land. However, the GROFTET variable is only available from 2016 onwards. Thus, the first of the two reporting periods (2015-2019) only includes 4 of 5 years in a full NFI cycle (cf. Section 9 below).

2.2 Sampling design

There are about 12 000 NFI plots in forest ecosystems, distributed on a regular grid, with varying grid density for forest below/above the coniferous forest line and in Finnmark county (Viken 2025). The indicator is based on observations from NFI, where ditching since 2016 has been recorded within a 0.1 ha observation area surrounding each 250 m2 inventory plot. In most cases, the observation area is circular with a radius of 17.8 m. However, where stand boundaries or other land-cover types intersect this distance, the shape of the observation area is adjusted while maintaining the total area of 0.1 ha.

Drainage ditches are recorded if present within the observation area. Because ditches may occur along stand boundaries or adjacent land-cover types, they can be located at the edge of the observation area. When a ditch forms the boundary between two forest stands, it is assigned to the stand for which it is considered to have the greatest drainage effect.

2.3 Original units

At plot level, the NFI variable groftet uses code 1 for ditched plots and code 2 for not ditched (intact water table). Only plots with non-missing groftet codes 1 or 2 are used; missing values are excluded. Aggregated values are the area-weighted mean proportion of area with intact water table.

2.4 Instructions for citing, using and accessing data

The data used for this indicator have been made available by the Norwegian National Forest Inventory under a specific data use agreement of 2026-05-29, between NIBIO and the specified users. Further use of these data requires a separate data user agreement with NIBIO.

3. Indicator properties

3.1 Ecosystem Condition Typology Class (ECT)

A1 Physical State Characteristics

3.2 Ecosystem condition characteristic

The indicator represents the area proportion of the forest with intact hydrology.

3.3 Collinearities with other indicators

None of the other indicators describe the same aspect of ecological status as this indicator.

3.4 Impact factors

The most important impact factors affecting the water table in peatland forests, swamp forests and wet forests on mineral soil is drainage for forest production, agriculture or road construction.

3.5 Spatial aggregation

The spatial aggregation pathway is: aggregate (NFI plots to county) – [scale] – aggregate (geographic region) – aggregate (national level). Non-scaled values are area-weighted means of the plot-level indicator (share of area with intact water table). Scaling uses national reference levels X0 = 0% and X100 = 100% intact water table area share (proportions 0 and 1 on the original scale).

4. Reference condition and levels

4.1 Reference condition

The reference condition is considered to be intact water levels in forest ecosystems where effects of human activities are clearly subordinate to those of natural ecosystem processes and disturbances.

4.2 Reference levels

X0 is set to 0% intact water table area share (worst condition) and X100 to 100% intact water table area share (best condition). Values are scaled linearly between X0 and X100 (Section 9.4).

4.2.1 Spatial resolution and validity

National reference levels are applied when scaling each county before regional and national aggregation.

5. Uncertainties

5.1 General uncertainties

The reported uncertainty reflects sampling variability but does not account for potential errors in field observations or ecological uncertainty. In particular, the indicator records the presence of drainage ditches rather than their current hydrological effects and assumes that presence of ditches is an adequate proxy for altered water-table conditions. It does not account for ditch characteristics, precise location or variation in the ditches’ hydrological effects. Observer judgement in recording ditches and assigning boundary ditches, as well as uncertainty in the definition of the area basis, may also influence estimates. While the intention of omitting certain forest areas was to include only forest areas where ditching has a documented effect on the water table and the species composition, the omittance criteria needs to be developed to better meet these intensions.

5.2 Reported uncertainties

County non-scaled values are bootstrapped with 1000 draws per county per reporting period. County draws are linearly scaled using the reference levels X0 and X100. Uncertainty is propagated when aggregating to regional and national levels.

6. References

Viken, K. O. 2025. Landsskogtakseringens Feltinstruks — 2025. Vol. 7. 5. NIBIO Bok.

7. Import and prepare datasets

7.1 Ditching

The NFI records ditching status in groftet (code 1 = ditched, code 2 = not ditched). Mesic, moist or wet forest types where ditching may affect drainage are listed in the area basis table (data/ArealGrunnlag_Groftet_Drenerbart_areal.csv; column arealgrunnlag_groftet_final == 1 for combinations of veg_type and vegtype_fukt).

Load data 
lsk_data_file <- here("data/LSK_data_okologisk_tilstand_2026-06-03.csv")
areal_grunnlag_file <- here("data/ArealGrunnlag_Groftet_Drenerbart_areal.csv")

lsk_data_raw <- read_csv(
  lsk_data_file,
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8")
) |>
  clean_names()

areal_grunnlag <- read_delim(
  areal_grunnlag_file,
  delim = ";",
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8", decimal_mark = ".", grouping_mark = ",")
) |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    veg_type_fukt = VegType_Fukt,
    is_ditchable = ArealGrunnlag_GROFTET_final == 1L
  )

8. Spatial units

Aggregated indicator values are reported for counties, regions and nationally. Where counties are split into parts, those parts are merged to a single county name before aggregation. Aggregated indicator values should not be applied for spatial units finer than those that allow reliable estimates.

9. Analyses

9.1 Prepare data and create derived variables

This section prepares the NFI plot data for indicator calculation. We define five-year reporting periods for 2015–2019 and 2020–2024.

Define reporting periods 
reporting_periods <- list(
  "2015-2019" = 2015:2019,
  "2020-2024" = 2020:2024
)

We retain plots with groftet codes 1 or 2 and attach is_ditchable from the area-basis table (arealgrunnlag_groftet_final). Split NFI county labels are standardised by merging county parts to a single county name. For each plot, is_ditched is 1 when groftet == 1 and 0 otherwise. Area-weighted contributions are ditched_weighted = is_ditched * au_areal and ditchable_area_weighted = is_ditchable * au_areal.

Create derived variables 
lsk_data <- lsk_data_raw |>
  mutate(veg_type_fukt = paste0(veg_type, "-", vegtype_fukt)) |>
  dplyr::left_join(areal_grunnlag, by = "veg_type_fukt") |>
  dplyr::mutate(is_ditchable = dplyr::coalesce(is_ditchable, FALSE)) |>
  filter(!is.na(groftet), groftet %in% c(1, 2)) |>
  mutate(
    fylke_name = region_fylke |>
      stringr::str_remove("^\\d+-\\d+\\s+") |>
      stringr::str_remove("^\\d+\\s*") |>
      stringr::str_squish(),
    fylke_name = normalize_county_parts(fylke_name),
    fylke_name_clean = stringi::stri_trans_general(
      stringr::str_squish(
        tolower(stringr::str_replace_all(fylke_name, "[0-9.-]", " "))
      ),
      "Latin-ASCII"
    ),
    region_code = assign_region_code(fylke_name_clean),
    region_name = assign_region_name(region_code),
    is_ditched = groftet == 1L,
    ditched_weighted = is_ditched * au_areal,
    ditchable_area_weighted = is_ditchable * au_areal
  )

Finally, we retain only the columns required for subsequent aggregation and reporting.

Select downstream fields 
lsk_data <- lsk_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    sesong,
    au_areal,
    arealtype,
    region_code,
    region_name,
    fylke_name,
    fylke_name_clean,
    veg_type,
    vegtype_fukt,
    veg_type_fukt,
    groftet,
    is_ditched,
    is_ditchable,
    ditched_weighted,
    ditchable_area_weighted
  )

9.2 Calculate non-scaled area-weighted indicator values

For each reporting period, we calculate values separately for counties, regions, and Norway. Within each geographic unit, we first sum plot-level area weights across all assessed plots (sum(au_areal)).

The non-scaled variable ditched_share is the share of that total area that is ditched: we add ditched_weighted across plots and divide by total assessed area.

The lower reference ditchable_share (X0) is calculated by dividing ditchable_area_weighted by total assessed area. This is the share of total assessed area where forest types can be ditched (is_ditchable). This is the maximum ditched share the unit could reach if all ditchable forest were ditched. Both ditched_share and ditchable_share are proportions on the 0–1 scale.

The scaled indicator value (0–1) is then 1 - ditched_share / ditchable_share when ditchable_share > 0 (implemented in scale_aivs_value() via ecTools::ec_normalise()). Point estimates of ditched_share, ditchable_share, and scaled_value are returned by calculate_aivs_period() via summarise_aivs().

Calculate non-scaled values 
aivs_results <- purrr::imap(
  reporting_periods,
  ~ calculate_aivs_period(lsk_data, .y, .x)
) |>
  purrr::compact()

aivs_results_county_all_periods   <- purrr::map_dfr(aivs_results, "county")
aivs_results_region_all_periods   <- purrr::map_dfr(aivs_results, "region")
aivs_results_national_all_periods <- purrr::map_dfr(aivs_results, "national")

9.3 Bootstrap draws per county

For each county and period, NFI plots are resampled with replacement 1000 times. Each bootstrap replicate recalculates ditched_share and ditchable_share from the resampled plots and applies the same scaling rule as in §9.2 (scaled_value = 1 - ditched_share / ditchable_share for that replicate). The resulting scaled_value draws are used for county uncertainty and for ec_upscale() in §9.4.

Bootstrap draws per county (1000 per county per period) 
county_draws_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(aivs_results, function(period_result, period_name) {
  county_meta <- period_result$county |>
    dplyr::select(fylke_name, total_area, region_code)

  purrr::imap_dfr(
    split(period_result$data, period_result$data$fylke_name),
    function(county_data, county_name) {
      boot <- bootstrap_aivs(county_data, n_bootstrap = 1000)
      dplyr::tibble(
        period            = period_name,
        fylke_name        = county_name,
        sim_id            = seq_along(boot$draws),
        ditched_share     = boot$draws,
        ditchable_share   = boot$ditchable_share_draws,
        scaled_value      = boot$scaled_draws
      )
    }
  ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(county_meta, by = "fylke_name")
})

The table below (Table 2) shows the area-weighted share of ditched forest per county for all reporting periods, with approximate 95% bootstrap confidence intervals (2.5th–97.5th percentile of 1000 draws).

9.4 Scale indicator values and aggregate to regional and national levels

Reference levels are X100 = 0% ditched (ditched_share = 0) and a unit-specific X0 = ditchable_share (when all ditchable forest in the unit is ditched). Scaling uses the linear relationship in §9.2; values outside [0, 1] are truncated at scaling when ditched_share exceeds ditchable_share. Each county’s 1000 bootstrap draws from section 9.3 already include scaled_value; §9.4 does not apply a separate scaling step. Figure 1 shows the mapping for a sample of draws.

Prepare scaled county draws and county uncertainty summaries 
county_draws_scaled <- county_draws_long |>
  dplyr::mutate(nation = "National")

county_scaled_with_uncertainty <- county_draws_scaled |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, fylke_name, total_area, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median = median(scaled_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    p_025  = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)),
    p_25   = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)),
    p_75   = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)),
    p_975  = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    aivs_results_county_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, fylke_name, n_plots),
    by = c("period", "fylke_name")
  )
Normalisation diagnostic figure 
norm_plot_data <- county_draws_scaled |>
  dplyr::group_by(fylke_name) |>
  dplyr::group_modify(~ dplyr::slice_sample(.x, n = min(100, nrow(.x)))) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

aivs_norm_plot <- ec_norm_plot(
  norm_plot_data$ditched_share,
  norm_plot_data$scaled_value
) +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ norm_plot_data$fylke_name, scales = "free_x") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Ditched area share (proportion)",
    y = "Scaled indicator value"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme(axis.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 9))
aivs_norm_plot$layers[[1]]$aes_params$size <- 3

suppressMessages(
  ggsave(
    filename = here::here("img/aivs_norm_plot.png"),
    plot = aivs_norm_plot,
    scale = 1.5
  )
)

aivs_norm_plot

The scaled draws are then passed to ecTools::ec_upscale(), which propagates uncertainty through the spatial hierarchy. For each of 1000 Monte Carlo iterations, one draw is sampled per county and the area-weighted mean gives one regional value; a second ec_upscale() call repeats this from regions to national level.

ec_upscale: county to region (compute once, save RDS) 
aivs_mc_cache_deps <- c(
  lsk_data_file,
  areal_grunnlag_file,
  here::here("R/functions.R"),
  here::here("R/NO_AIVS_001.qmd")
)
aivs_mc_rds_regional <- here::here("data/aivs_mc_regional.rds")

if (
  file.exists(aivs_mc_rds_regional) &&
  file.mtime(aivs_mc_rds_regional) >= max(file.mtime(aivs_mc_cache_deps), na.rm = TRUE)
) {
  aivs_mc_regional <- readRDS(aivs_mc_rds_regional)
} else {
  aivs_mc_regional <- ecTools::ec_upscale(
    data        = county_draws_scaled,
    variable    = scaled_value,
    weight      = total_area,
    start_units = fylke_name,
    end_units   = region_code,
    year        = period,
    end_units_name = "region_code",
    n = 1000
  )
  saveRDS(aivs_mc_regional, aivs_mc_rds_regional)
}
ec_upscale: region to national (compute once, save RDS) 
region_area_weights <- county_draws_scaled |>
  dplyr::distinct(period, fylke_name, total_area, region_code) |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(region_area = sum(total_area), .groups = "drop")

aivs_mc_regional_for_national <- aivs_mc_regional |>
  dplyr::left_join(region_area_weights, by = c("period", "region_code")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(nation = "National")

aivs_mc_rds_national <- here::here("data/aivs_mc_national.rds")

if (
  file.exists(aivs_mc_rds_national) &&
  file.mtime(aivs_mc_rds_national) >= max(
    c(file.mtime(aivs_mc_cache_deps), file.mtime(aivs_mc_rds_regional)),
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
) {
  aivs_mc_national <- readRDS(aivs_mc_rds_national)
} else {
  aivs_mc_national <- ecTools::ec_upscale(
    data        = aivs_mc_regional_for_national,
    variable    = sampled_mean,
    weight      = region_area,
    start_units = region_code,
    end_units   = nation,
    year        = period,
    end_units_name = "nation",
    n = 1000
  )
  saveRDS(aivs_mc_national, aivs_mc_rds_national)
}
Combine regional and national MC draws; build summary tables 
aivs_mc_combined <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  aivs_mc_regional |> dplyr::select(period, region = region_code, sampled_mean),
  aivs_mc_national |> dplyr::select(period, region = nation, sampled_mean)
)

county_scaled_all_periods <- aivs_results_county_all_periods |>
  dplyr::select(period, fylke_name, scaled_value, ditchable_share, ditched_share)

region_scaled_agg_all_periods <- aivs_mc_regional |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(scaled_value = median(sampled_mean), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(region_name = assign_region_name(region_code))

national_scaled_agg_all_periods <- aivs_mc_national |>
  dplyr::group_by(period) |>
  dplyr::summarise(scaled_value = median(sampled_mean), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(region = "National")

region_scaled_agg_with_uncertainty <- aivs_mc_regional |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median = median(sampled_mean),
    p_025  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.025)),
    p_25   = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.25)),
    p_75   = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.75)),
    p_975  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.975)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(region_name = assign_region_name(region_code)) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    aivs_results_region_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, region_code, total_area, n_plots),
    by = c("period", "region_code")
  )

national_scaled_agg_with_uncertainty <- aivs_mc_national |>
  dplyr::group_by(period) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median = median(sampled_mean),
    p_025  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.025)),
    p_25   = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.25)),
    p_75   = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.75)),
    p_975  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.975)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(region = "National") |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    aivs_results_national_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, total_area, n_plots),
    by = "period"
  )

Truncation may apply when the ditched area share exceeds the unit-specific ditchable_share (X0); scaled values outside [0, 1] are truncated at scaling.

The figure below (Figure 2) summarises regional and national indicator values by reporting period.

Time series of indicator values by region and period 
aivs_region_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Øst", "2" = "Sør", "3" = "Vest",
  "4" = "Midt", "5" = "Nord", "National" = "National"
)
aivs_region_levels <- c("National", "Midt", "Nord", "Øst", "Sør", "Vest")
aivs_region_cols <- setNames(
  MetBrewer::met.brewer("Archambault", n = length(aivs_region_levels)),
  aivs_region_levels
)

aivs_plot_data <- aivs_mc_combined |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    region_name = dplyr::recode(region, !!!aivs_region_labels),
    series_type = dplyr::if_else(region == "National", "National", "Region")
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_name, series_type) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median = median(sampled_mean),
    p_025  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.025)),
    p_975  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.975)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(region_name = factor(region_name, levels = aivs_region_levels))

ggplot2::ggplot(
  aivs_plot_data,
  ggplot2::aes(x = period, y = median, colour = region_name, group = region_name)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(
    ggplot2::aes(linewidth = series_type),
    position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.18)
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_pointrange(
    ggplot2::aes(ymin = p_025, ymax = p_975, shape = series_type),
    size = 0.8, linewidth = 0.45,
    position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.18)
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = aivs_region_cols, name = NULL) +
  ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(values = c(National = 19, Region = 17), name = NULL) +
  ggplot2::scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(National = 1, Region = 0.55), guide = "none") +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.box         = "horizontal",
    panel.grid.minor   = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = ggplot2::element_blank()
  ) +
  ggplot2::guides(
    colour = ggplot2::guide_legend(
      nrow = 2,
      override.aes = list(size = 0.7, linewidth = 0.45)
    ),
    shape  = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 0.8, linewidth = 0.45))
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(y = "Indicator value", x = "")

10. Results

The following tables present scaled indicator results at county, regional, and national scales by reporting period. Tables report the median as the indicator value, with an approximate 95% interval (2.5% and 97.5% quantiles) and an approximate interquartile range (25% and 75% quantiles). Scaled values are on a 0–1 scale, where 1 corresponds to 0% ditched area share (X100) and 0 corresponds to a ditched area share equal to the unit-specific ditchable forest share (X0).

10.1 County-level results (scaled with uncertainty)

10.2 Regional results (scaled with uncertainty)

10.3 National results (scaled with uncertainty)

11. Export file

Regional and national Monte Carlo draws are exported in one table (aivs_mc_combined: period, region, sampled_mean; region contains the five NI region codes and "National").

Export regional and national MC-propagated indicator draws 
write.csv(
  aivs_mc_combined,
  here::here("data/results_NO_AIVS_001.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

MeRIT attribution

This part supplements the CRediT statement with more granularity for method section.

Study design The study was designed by [AB, CD].

Data collection / fieldwork Field data were collected by [AB, EF] between [years]. Sampling protocol was developed by [CD].

Laboratory procedures DNA extraction was performed by [GH]. Sequencing was conducted by [IJ].

Data processing Raw data were curated and cleaned by [EF]. Code for preprocessing was written by [KL].

Statistical analysis All analyses were conducted by [CD] using [software], with verification by [AB].

Visualization Figures were produced by [KL], with input from [CD].

Reproducibility All code was reviewed and validated by [AB, CD].

TipSession Info

This information is mainly aimed at developers that need to recreate the R environment that produced the html. Important note: this information is about the R session that was running when the html was rendered, and if some of the code chunks were not evaluated when the html was rendered, they may have been executed in a different environment.
